channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: Marco Rubio confirmed by Senate to be next secretary of state, becomes first Trump cabinet pick to be approved
item.description: Florida Sen. Marco Rubio was confirmed by the Senate to be the next secretary of state Monday night, making him the first of President Trump's Cabinet picks to receive congressional approval.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Inside the intimate inauguration: Close-up encounters between political rivals, some awkward
item.description: A president’s inaugural address is a time-honored spectacle: A makeshift grandstand is erected next to the Capitol and hundreds of thousands people line the National Mall.
item.dc:creator: Farnoush Amiri, Stephen Groves, Julia Nikhinson, And Mary Clare Jalonick, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Trump did not place hand on Bibles during 2025 swearing in
item.description: President Trump did not place his hands on a stack of Bibles while taking the oath of office Monday, but he did not violate the U.S. Constitution either.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: At Trump's second inaugural, a changed Washington and a full embrace from Republicans
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Donald Trump took the oath of office on Monday inside a tightly packed Capitol Rotunda, where he was surrounded by a very different Washington than he was eight years ago.
item.dc:creator: MARY CLARE JALONICK, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump names acting defense secretary with Hegseth not yet confirmed
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Donald Trump has named an acting defense secretary because his choice to lead the Pentagon, Pete Hegseth, has not yet been confirmed by the Senate.
item.dc:creator: TARA COPP and LOLITA C. BALDOR, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Former presidents mum following Donald Trump's inauguration
item.description: The men who held the nation’s highest office before President Trump have all remained mum on his taking the presidency on Monday.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Biden pardons his siblings and their spouses on his way out of the White House
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Joe Biden pardoned his siblings and their spouses on his way out of the White House, saying Monday that his family had been “subjected to unrelenting attacks and threats, motivated solely by a desire to hurt me — the worst kind of partisan politics.
item.dc:creator: ZEKE MILLER, COLLEEN LONG and ALANNA DURKIN RICHER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: What Melania Trump wore to the inauguration
item.description: While red baseball caps have become synonymous with President Donald Trump, first lady Melania Trump made her own millinery-related fashion statement, sporting a navy wide-brimmed hat by an American designer on Inauguration Day.
item.dc:creator: BEATRICE DUPUY, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Border app that became 'a salvation' for migrants to legally enter the US may end
item.description: TIJUANA, Mexico (AP) — A nurse who fled Cuba as part of the Caribbean nation’s largest exodus in more than six decades needed a place to stay in Mexico as she waited to legally enter the U.S. using a government app.
item.dc:creator: ELLIOT SPAGAT, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump officials shut down CBP One app for migrants
item.description: The app shutdown is part of a series of moves by the incoming administration to crack down on the border.
item.dc:creator: Rafael Bernal, NewsNation

item.title: Florida officials react to Trump inauguration
item.description: Donald Trump was sworn in as the 47th President of the United States on Monday. Here's how Florida officials are responding.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Tucker, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Trump national security adviser pick Waltz resigns from House, shrinking GOP majority
item.description: Trump tapped Waltz, a House lawmaker and former Green Beret, in the fall to serve as his national security adviser.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Mark Milley pardoned: General at center of Afghanistan withdrawal predicted it wouldn't be a Saigon moment
item.description: President Biden issued a preemptive pardon to Gen. Mark Milley on Monday, capping off a presidency marred by the chaotic Afghanistan withdrawal in 2021.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Here are all executive orders Trump is expected to sign on Day 1
item.description: An executive order is a signed statement about how the president wants the federal government to be managed.
item.dc:creator: Safia Samee Ali, NewsNation

item.title: Trump declares ‘Golden Age’ in America in inaugural address
item.description: President Trump on Monday declared it was the beginning of a new “Golden Age" in America, pledging to restore a country that he said had been decimated by the previous administration as he officially took the oath of office for his second White House term.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, NewsNation

item.title: Inauguration Day Latest: Trump expected to begin signing first round of executive orders
item.description: Donald Trump has been sworn in as the 47th President of the United States, taking charge as Republicans claim unified control of Washington and set out to reshape the country’s institutions.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Melania dons hat at Trump inauguration, struggles to kiss Trump
item.description: First lady Melania Trump wore a navy and ivory ensemble by American designer Adam Lippes for the inauguration ceremony.
item.dc:creator: Patrick Djordjevic, NewsNation

item.title: Indigenous activist Leonard Peltier granted clemency by President Biden
item.description: The outgoing administration has released Leonard Peltier from prison. Numerous activists and tribal officials have requested the release of Peltier, whom they believe to be innocent of killing two FBI agents in 1975.
item.dc:creator: Allison Herrera and Melissa Olson, MPR News

item.title: Biden commutes sentence for Indigenous activist Leonard Peltier, convicted in killing of FBI agents
item.description: President Joe Biden has commuted the sentence of Indigenous activist Leonard Peltier, who was convicted in the 1975 killings of two FBI agents and is serving life in prison.
item.dc:creator: Colleen Long And Zeke Miller, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Veteran agent named as acting FBI director pending Senate confirmation of Trump's pick for post
item.description: The White House has named a veteran agent to serve as acting FBI director after the official who’d been expected to run the bureau on an interim basis following the departure of Christopher Wray retired after nearly 30 years.
item.dc:creator: Eric Tucker, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Trump expected to sign these executive orders related to the border
item.description: Trump is expected to declare a national emergency at the southern border, designate cartels as global terrorists and end birthright citizenship.
item.dc:creator: Kellie Meyer, NewsNation

item.title: MLK Jr.'s daughter: 'Wonderful' Trump being inaugurated on day celebrating father
item.description: Bernice King, the daughter of late civil rights leader Martin Luther King Jr., says the simultaneous occurrence of MLK Day and Inauguration Day this year is an opportunity to reflect on her father's legacy.
item.dc:creator: Cheyanne M. Daniels, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Moving presidents in, out 'mass bedlam': Former White House usher
item.description: Emery said the process of moving presidents in and out of the White House “is almost like Cirque du Soleil.
item.dc:creator: Kevin Bohn, NewsNation

item.title: Trump expected to sign record number of executive orders on Monday
item.description: "We are going to sign them at the beginning … we are doing them tomorrow,” Trump said at a Sunday rally.
item.dc:creator: Joe Khalil, NewsNation

item.title: Trump returns to Washington for celebrations ahead of inauguration
item.description: Donald Trump returned to Washington Saturday evening to celebrate with family, supporters and political allies ahead of his second inauguration.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Civil rights leaders and King family mark MLK Day as a special call to action as Trump takes office
item.description: Events honoring Martin Luther King Jr.’s legacy and advocating for his vision of a just, nonviolent society will occur the same day as Donald Trump's second presidential inauguration.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Biden won't enforce TikTok ban, official says, leaving fate of app to Trump
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Joe Biden won't enforce a ban on the social media app TikTok that is set to take effect a day before he leaves office on Monday, a U.S. official said Thursday, leaving its fate in the hands of President-elect Donald Trump.
item.dc:creator: ZEKE MILLER, JOSH BOAK, MICHELLE L. PRICE and MARY CLARE JALONICK, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: No taxes on tips comes closer to reality with Cortez Masto, Rosen push
item.description: No taxes on tips is becoming a closer reality for millions of workers after Democratic Nevada Sens. Catherine Cortez Masto and Jacky Rosen and other senators reintroduced the proposal Thursday, her office said.
item.dc:creator: David Charns, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Cortez Masto leads Nevada Democrats in renewed effort against nuclear waste dump
item.description: Nevada's Democratic U.S. senators and representatives in Congress reintroduced the Nuclear Waste Informed Consent Act on Wednesday, gearing up for possible renewed efforts to put a nuclear dump in the state.
item.dc:creator: Greg Haas, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Pam Bondi grilled over Trump's influence at AG hearing: 5 takeaways
item.description: Pam Bondi, President-elect Trump’s pick to lead the Department of Justice, echoed attacks on the agency she would lead if confirmed, but demurred on how she might handle tests of its independence. Bondi is a more traditional pick to lead the department than Trump’s initial choice, former Rep.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Florida's Rubio and Bondi face confirmation hearings
item.description: Former state attorney general and Tampa native Pam Bondi and Senator Marco Rubio are set to appear on Capitol Hill for confirmation hearings, Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: Beth Rousseau, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Capitol chaos: DeSantis faces backlash from Florida lawmakers over special session
item.description: Desantis says the state needs new laws to help President Trump carry out his immigration reforms in Florida. But there's some disagreement over that special session.
item.dc:creator: Mackenzie LaPorte, WFLA, NBC
